feature.name: Bard Subclass: College of Dolce Stil Novo (HB)
feature.text:
Weave magic through their poetic language and transcendent visions
E io a lui: «I' mi son un che, quando Amor mi spira, noto, e a quel modo ch'e' ditta dentro vo significando». «O frate, issa vegg'io», diss'elli, «il nodo che 'l Notaro e Guittone e me ritenne di qua dal dolce stil novo ch'i' odo! — Divine Comedy, Purgatory XXIV, vv. 52-57
Theme: This subclass connects directly to the Dolce Stil Novo's ideals of love and divine beauty as transcendent forces. Each feature reflects the poets' ability to inspire and captivate both allies and adversaries.
The name ('sweet new style') comes from a dialogue in Dante's Purgatorio (xxiv. 57). This style is marked mainly by praise of the beloved in religious terms as an embodiment of divine grace, an attitude seemingly influenced by troubadour poetry and the codes of courtly love.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 11 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 3: Poetic Transcendence (College of Dolce Stil Novo (HB))
feature.text:
You channel your bardic magic through refined and metaphysical poetry, inspiring profound thoughts and feelings.
Inspiration of Love. When a creature uses your Bardic Inspiration die, it regains hit points equal to the number rolled + your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1).
Stanza of Enlightenment. As a Bonus Action, you can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to recite a mystical stanza targeting one creature that can hear you within 30 feet of you. Choose one effect:
• Hearts Opened. The target has Advantage on Charisma Tests until the end of your next turn.
• Veil Lifted. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. Failure: Disadvantage on its next Test.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 11 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 3: Voice of the Muse (College of Dolce Stil Novo (HB))
feature.text:
Your connection to divine inspiration sharpens your eloquence and insight. You gain proficiency in Insight or Performance, and one additional language of your choice. You gain Expertise in one of these skills if you are already proficient in both.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 11 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 6: Visionary Verse (College of Dolce Stil Novo (HB))
feature.text:
Your poetry channels visions of divine beauty and metaphysical truth that inspire or bewilder your audience. As a Magic Action, you can weave a Visionary Verse. Choose up to three creatures within 60 feet of you that can hear you:
• Your Allies gain Temporary Hit Points equal to twice your Bard level and have Advantage on saving throws against the Frightened and Charmed conditions for 1 minute.
• Creatures Hostile to you make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. Failure: Blinded condition until the end of their next turn.
Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again until you finish a Long Rest.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 11 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 14: Divine Illumination (College of Dolce Stil Novo (HB))
feature.text:
Channeling the power of pure beauty and divine inspiration, as a Magic Action you invoke the highest ideals of the Dolce Stil Novo poets, that take the form of a 15-foot Emanation around you:
Aura of Inspiration. While in the Emanation, your Allies are Immune to Psychic Damage and gain a bonus to their saving throws equal to your Charisma modifier. When the Emanation enters an Hostile creature's space and whenever that creature enters the Emanation for the first time in a turn, or starts its turn there, that creature makes a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC. Failure: Disadvantage on any Tests until the end of its next turn.
Final Canticle. As a Magic Action, you can recite a canticle of divine love, ending the Emanation. Choose up to six creatures within 60 feet that can hear you. Your Allies regain Hit Points equal to 5d10 + your Bard level up to their maximum, and end one Condition affecting them. Hostile creatures make a DC Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. Failure: 6d10 Psychic damage and the Blinded condition until the end of their next turn. Success: half as much damage and no other ill effects on a successful save.
Once you use this feature, you can't use them again until you finish a Long Rest.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 11 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Bard Subclass: College of Skalds (HB)
feature.text:
Drawing power from epic tales
Theme: Norse warrior-poets who inspire allies with tales of heroism and channel primal rage and magic.
Playstyle: Martial and supportive. Combines combat prowess with powerful rallying abilities for a battlefield leader role.
Skalds, or skálds (meaning "poet" in Old Norse), were poets who composed skaldic poetry, one of the two kinds of Old Norse poetry in alliterative verse, the other being Eddic poetry. Skaldic poems were traditionally composed to honor kings, but were sometimes ex tempore. They include both extended works and single verses.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 9 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 3: Skald's Inspiration (College of Skalds (HB))
feature.text:
When you join the College of the Skald at 3rd level, your Bardic Inspiration takes on a martial edge. You gain the following benefits:
Battle Chant. When a creature uses your Bardic Inspiration die, they can choose to deal extra damage equal to the die roll on a weapon attack or gain Temporary Hit Points equal to the roll.
Tales of the Ancestors. You learn one additional spell from the Bard Spell List. This spell must be of a level you can cast, and it doesn't count against your spells known.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 9 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 3: Words of Fury (College of Skalds (HB))
feature.text:
You can stir the hearts of your Allies into a battle frenzy. As a Bonus Action, you can use your Bardic Inspiration to target up to five creatures that you can see within 30 feet of you. These creatures gain one of the following benefits of your choice until the start of your next turn:
• Advantage on attack rolls. • A +2 bonus to their AC
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 9 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 6: Saga of Heroes (College of Skalds (HB))
feature.text:
You invoke the tales of legendary Norse warriors to inspire greatness in your allies. You have the following
Saga's Guidance. As a Magic Action, you can choose up to 3 creatures within 60 feet of you. Each creature gains Temporary Hit points equal to twice your Bard level and can add your Charisma modifier to one Test of their choice within the next minute.
Warding Runes. When a creature that is Allied to you that you can see within 10 feet of you takes damage, you can use your Reaction to reduce the damage by 1d10 + your Charisma modifier.
You can use this feature twice, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 9 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 14: Valkyries' Call (College of Skalds (HB))
feature.text:
As a Bonus Action, You call upon the Valkyries, the legendary spirits of battle, that flit around you in a 30-foot Emanation that lasts for 1 minute. While in the Emanation:
• Your Allies have advantage on Death Saving Throws and saving throws against the Frightened condition.
• When your Allies take damage, they can use their Reaction to move up to 15 feet and make a single weapon attack.
• Hostile Creatures have Disadvantage on attack rolls.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 9 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Bard Subclass: College of the Cabaret (HB)
feature.text:
Extravagant master performer
Theme: Flamboyant and provocative performers who beguile and dazzle through sharp wit and charm.
Playstyle: Battlefield control and charm. Uses subversive magic to hinder enemies while uplifting allies.
Referencing the period between 1871 to 1914, La Belle Époque literally means "the beautiful era" in French. In more ways than one, La Belle Époque has been regarded as Europe's golden age, a remarkable time that significantly altered the history of the continent and beyond. The Cabaret is a form of theatrical entertainment featuring music, song, dance, recitation, or drama. The performance venue might be a pub, a casino, a hotel, a restaurant, or a nightclub with a stage for performances. The audience, often dining or drinking, does not typically dance but usually sits at tables.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 16 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 3: Dazzling Presence (College of the Cabaret (HB))
feature.text:
Your performances captivate and confuse onlookers.
Flamboyant Wit. When you cast a spell that targets a creature, you can use your Bonus Action to distract another creature that can see you within 30 feet of you. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. Failure: Disadvantage on its Tests until the end of your next turn.
Performance Expertise. You gain Expertise in Performance, and you can use Charisma (Performance) checks in place of Dexterity (Stealth) when attempting to blend into a crowd.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 16 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 6: Revelry's Charm (College of the Cabaret (HB))
feature.text:
Your revelry bolsters allies and disorients foes. As a Bonus Action, you can affect up to 6 creatures that can hear or see you within 60 feet of you.
• For 1 minute, your Allies gain temporary hit points equal to your Bardic Inspiration die at the start of each of their turns.
• For 1 minute, at the start of each of their turns, creatures Hostile to you must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. Failure: Disadvantage on any attack roll until the end of their turn.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest. You can also restore your use of it by expending a 3+ level spell slot (no action required).
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 16 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 14: Grand Finale (College of the Cabaret (HB))
feature.text:
Your ultimate performance leaves audiences awestruck. As a Magic Action, you unleash a performance that affects all creatures of your choice within 60 feet of you that can hear or see you:
• Your Allies gain 5d10 temporary hit points, can immediately make a weapon attack as a Reaction, and have Advantage on all saving throws for 1 minute.
• Creatures Hostile to you must make a Wisdom against your spell save DC. Failure: Stunned condition for 1 minute. At the end of each of its next turns, an affected creature can make another Wisdom saving throw, ending the condition on itself on a success.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 16 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Bard Subclass: College of the Elizabethan Theatre (HB)
feature.text:
Enhance acting with magic
Theme: Every feature draws from the spirit of Elizabethan theatre, blending the emotions of tragedy and comedy with the grandeur of live performance.
Playstyle: Versatile performer. Its playstyle is a balance of social manipulation, adaptability, and combat trickery.
Elizabethan theatre, sometimes called English Renaissance theatre, refers to that style of performance plays which blossomed during the reign of Elizabeth I of England and which continued under her Stuart successors.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 13 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 3: Theatrical Persona (College of the Elizabethan Theatre (HB))
feature.text:
You adopt the larger-than-life personas of the stage to captivate and inspire.
Masks of the Stage. As a Bonus action, you can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to assume a theatrical persona, such as the Tragic Hero, the Comedic Fool, or the Noble Monarch, granting you the following benefits for 1 minute:
Tragic Hero. When you or a creature Allied to you that you can see within 10 feet of you takes damage, you can use your Reaction to reduce the damage by 1d6 + your Charisma modifier.
Comedic Fool. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. Failure: Disadvantage on their next Test.
Noble Monarch. You and your Allies within 10 feet of you gain Advantage on saving throws against the Frightened and Charmed condition.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 13 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 3: Wordsmith's Wit (College of the Elizabethan Theatre (HB))
feature.text:
Your mastery of dialogue and soliloquy sharpens your ability to manipulate social situations.
• You gain Proficiency in Deception or Persuasion. You gain Expertise in one of these skills if you are already proficient in both.
• When you cast a level 1+ spell from the Enchantment Scholl that forces a creature to make a saving throw, you can use your Reaction to give that creature Disadvantage on the roll. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 13 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 6: Playwright's Performance (College of the Elizabethan Theatre (HB))
feature.text:
You bring the power of the stage to life, crafting moments of triumph or despair. As a Magic Action, you create a magical performance that affects up to a number of creatures equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1) within 30 feet. Choose one effect:
Tragedy. Creatures of your choice that can see or hear you must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. Failure: 3d8 Psychic damage and Frightened condition until the end of your next turn. Success: Half damage.
Comedy. Creatures of your choice that can hear or see you regain hit points equal to 2d8 + your Charisma modifier and gain temporary hit points equal to your Bard level.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest. You can also restore your use of it by expending a 3+ level spell slot (no action required).
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 13 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 14: Stage of Destiny (College of the Elizabethan Theatre (HB))
feature.text:
You create an ethereal magical stage that manifests around you in a 15-foot Emanation that symbolizes themes of Elizabethan theatre to inspire or terrify those who witness it. The Emanation lasts for 1 minute.
Grand Performance. Within:
• Your Allies that can see the Emanation have Advantage on their Tests. • Creatures Hostile to you the Emanation have Disadvantage on their Tests.
Dramatic Finale. Once during this feature's duration, you can deliver a climactic soliloquy as a Magic Action. Choose one of the following effects:
• The Triumph: Your Allies within the Emanation regain 6d10 Hit Points and can immediately use their Reaction to move up to their speed without provoking opportunity attacks.
• The Fall: Creatures Hostile to you must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. Failure: 6d10 Psychic damage and Stunned condition until the end of your next turn. Success: Half damage.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 14 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Bard Subclass: College of the Minnesingers (HB)
feature.text:
Make extraordinary endeavors through love and magic
Theme: Romantic and spiritual devotion, uplifting allies through love and reverence.
Playstyle: Protective and defensive. Focuses on emotional bonds and resilience, heavily leaning into themes of chivalric love and faith.
Minnesingers flourished in the Middle-High German period (XII to XIV century). The name derives from Minne, the Middle High German word for love, as that was Minnesang's main subject. People who wrote and performed Minnesang were known as Minnesänger. and a single song was called a Minnelied. The Minnesänger are comparable to the Occitan troubadours and northern French trouvères, but they are "an original German contribution to courtly lyric."
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 15 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 3: Devotion's Blessing (College of the Minnesingers (HB))
feature.text:
Your words of love and reverence inspire resilience and determination.
Aura of Inspiration. When you confer a Bardic Inspiration die to a creature, that creature also gains temporary hit points equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1).
Song of Reverence. As a Bonus Action, you can perform a hymn of devotion. Choose one creature that can hear you within 30 feet of you. That creature gains Advantage on its next Text until the end of your next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 15 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 6: Eternal Love (College of the Minnesingers (HB))
feature.text:
Your devotion creates an unbreakable bond. When a creature Allied to you that you can see within 30 feet of you takes damage, you can use your Reaction to reduce the damage by an amount equal to your Bardic Inspiration die + your Charisma modifier. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 15 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 14: Minne's Transcendence (College of the Minnesingers (HB))
feature.text:
Your love and reverence transcend the material, uplifting allies and overwhelming foes.As Bonus action, you can sing a divine ode of love. Each creature of your choice that can hear you within 30 feet of you has the following effects:
• Your Allies have Advantage on saving throws and gain a bonus to attack rolls equal to your Charisma modifier for 1 minute.
• Hostile creatures must make a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC. Failure: Incapacitated condition for 1 minute. At the end of each of its next turns, an affected creature can make another Charisma saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 15 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Bard Subclass: College of the Troubadour (HB)
feature.text:
Dashing poet-musician in noble courts
Theme: Romantic poet-musicians of medieval France, weaving love and beauty into magic, embodying the spirit of medieval France's traveling poet-musicians, celebrated for their romantic ballads, satirical wit, and courtly presence.
Playstyle: Supportive and enchanting. Focuses on emotional connection and protective magic, with a hint of summoning.
Troubadours were composers and performesr of Old Occitan lyric poetry during the High Middle Ages (1100-1350 AD). The troubadour school or tradition began in the late 11th century in Occitania, but it subsequently spread to the Italian and Iberian Peninsulas. Under the influence of the troubadours, related movements sprang up throughout Europe. After the "classical" period around the turn of the 13th century and a mid-century resurgence, the art of the troubadours declined in the 14th century and around the time of the Black Death (1348) and since died out. The texts of troubadour songs deal mainly with themes of chivalry and courtly love. Most were metaphysical, intellectual, and formulaic. Many were humorous or vulgar satires.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 10 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 3: Ballad of Inspiration (College of the Troubadour (HB))
feature.text:
Your Bardic Inspiration becomes deeply emotional, evoking feelings of love, valor, or wit. You gain the following traits:
Chivalric Flai. When a creature uses your Bardic Inspiration die, it gains Temporary Hit Points equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1). Additionally, the creature can move up to 10 feet without provoking Opportunity Attacks.
Poet's Wit. When you use Bardic Inspiration, you can also target a Hostile creature within 30 feet of you. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. Failure: Disadvantage on its next attack roll or saving throw before the start of your next turn.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 10 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 3: Courtly Grace (College of the Troubadour (HB))
feature.text:
Your training in etiquette and charm allows you to manipulate social interactions. You gain Proficiency in Persuasion or Performance Skill. If you are already proficient, you gain Expertise in that skill. As a Reaction, when a creature that you can see within 30 feet of you makes an attack roll or saving throw, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration to roll the die and either add it to the roll or subtract it.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 10 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 6: Troubadour's Serenade (College of the Troubadour (HB))
feature.text:
You can entrance others with your music and poetry. As a Magic Action, you can perform a magical serenade that affects up to a number of creatures that can hear you equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1) within 30 feet of you. Each creature must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. Failure: Charmed condition for 1 minute or until it takes damage. A Charmed creature has also the Incapacitated condition, enraptured by your performance. At the end of each of its next turn, a Charmed creature can make another Wisdom saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 10 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 14: Hymn of Devotion (College of the Troubadour (HB))
feature.text:
Your songs and poetry channel a powerful magic that inspires loyalty and passion. As a Bonus Action, you can affect each creature of your choice other than you within 30 feet of you that can hear you. Target creatures are Immune to the Frightened and Charmed condition for 1 minute and gain 30 Temporary Hit Points at the start of each of their next turns for 1 minute. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 10 (Homebrew)
